items.title: Five arrests as police and protesters clash at Victoria Park amid Olympic stadium dispute
items.summary: Five people were taken into custody and a protest camp was broken up at Victoria Park ahead of construction beginning on the $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.

items.title: Afghan Refugee Women's XI Cricket Tour of Darwin spotlights need for gender equality in sport
items.summary: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

items.title: Jannik Sinner's French Open loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo leaves rivals stunned
items.summary: It seemed a foregone conclusion Jannik Sinner would win the French Open, which is why his meltdown amid the Paris heat wave was so unexpected.

items.title: New drivers inducted in Rockhampton for the last working Purrey steam tram in the world
items.summary: Steam-powered trams ran in Rockhampton for 30 years until 1939 and are now displayed at a volunteer museum, between their Sunday and school holiday trips.

items.title: Claims funding promise to cancer patients missing from Tasmanian state budget
items.summary: A cancer survivor who stood alongside the Tasmanian premier at a 2025 election promise announcement says he is disappointed that the hospitals investment appears to be "on the back burner."

items.title: Comedian Will Gibb on the perils of TikTok fame
items.summary: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

items.title: Sarah Gigante to miss Giro d'Italia Women through injury, Jai Hindley fourth in men's race with two stages remaining
items.summary: Sarah Gigante, who finished third at last year's Giro d'Italia Women, will not take to the start line on Saturday after succumbing to a leg injury, continuing her horror run of injury.

items.title: Diphtheria vaccine push in WA's north as authorities battle 'disease of the past' disinformation
items.summary: Health authorities in Western Australia's far north say they are battling disinformation and misconceptions amid a vaccination drive to contain a diphtheria outbreak.

items.title: ACCC sues Amazon over alleged non-compliance with button battery warnings in kids backpacks
items.summary: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon alleging it sold children's unicorn backpacks on its online marketplace that failed to comply with mandatory button battery safety warnings.

items.title: NSW skipper Isabelle Kelly 'speechless' after Blues make Women's State of Origin history
items.summary: NSW captain Isabelle Kelly praises her Blues teammates after they complete a historic 3-0 State of Origin whitewash against Queensland.

items.title: Yindjibarndi leader not ready to accept $150m native title payout from Andrew Forrest's Fortescue
items.summary: More than a fortnight after winning the largest native title compensation payout ever in Australia, Pilbara leader Michael Woodley returns to his country.

items.title: From Kneecap to Ecca Vandal and Genesis Owusu — these are May's best albums
items.summary: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

items.title: Auction clearance rates at 2022 housing downturn levels, ASX up — as it happened
items.summary: Auction clearance rates have fallen to levels not seen since the 2022 housing downturn. The ASX closed up 1.6 per cent on hopes of a Middle East peace deal.

items.title: Australian Border Force seize 20 million illegal vapes since January 2024 as crackdown continues
items.summary: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.

items.title: Controversial Sunshine Coast housing project receives federal approval
items.summary: Up to 12,000 homes could be added to the fast-growing region after a major hurdle was cleared for the contentious Aura South development to proceed.

items.title: Australia's Daria Kasatkina sets up French Open clash with Aryna Sabalenka in third round
items.summary: Daria Kasatkina will face world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the third round as fellow Australian Kimberly Birrell departs the singles draw in Paris.

items.title: Darwin Harbour Middle Arm gas and petrochemical precinct could be roadblocked by endangered herb
items.summary: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

items.title: Kids protest against fast-food giant after McDonald's buys Castlemaine land for $4.6m
items.summary: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.

items.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child in 2024
items.summary: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.

items.title: 'Eco village' project proposed as solution to Lismore housing crisis languishes
items.summary: It was lauded as a solution to the housing crisis, but those involved say plans to build an "eco village" in northern New South Wales have gone awry.

items.title: Jannik Sinner exits French Open in five-set loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo in Paris
items.summary: Jannik Sinner's French Open campaign is over after the world number one struggles with illness in a shock second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu directs Israel's military to take over 70 per cent of Gaza
items.summary: Israel effectively controls an estimated 64 per cent of Gaza, now bombarded to ruins by Israel's two-year military assault that followed the 2023 Hamas attack on southern Israel.

items.title: Celine Cremer search volunteers to look for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby
items.summary: A group of volunteers, which played a major role in finding the remains of a lost Belgian tourist earlier this year, will today begin searching for another missing person in Tasmania's wilderness.

items.title: Major wine retailer Endeavour Group retreats from production amid industry pressure
items.summary: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of Sydney and Melbourne jobs to India and Philippines
items.summary: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

items.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager
items.summary: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.

items.title: Patient advocates hope review will improve travel subsidy rates and response times
items.summary: Kevin O'Neill says Queensland's patient travel subsidy scheme left him stranded with a dangerous medical condition 300 kilometres from home. Patient advocates say it is one of a litany of failures they hope will be fixed in a review of the scheme.

items.title: Australia focused on making winning start at Valladolid leg of World Championship rugby sevens
items.summary: The threat of relegation hangs over the Australian men's rugby sevens squad ahead of the final two rounds of the World Championship series.

items.title: Private landholders hand three blocks in Tasmania to Aboriginal Land Council
items.summary: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.

items.title: Aggreko building Australia's largest hybrid power plant in outback Queensland in light of CopperString delays
items.summary: An international energy company is building Australia's largest off-grid renewable hybrid power facility, as a state politician criticises the need for it.

items.title: Austrian man jailed for 15 years for Taylor Swift concert terror plot
items.summary: A man who plotted but ultimately failed to launch a terrorist attack at Taylor Swift concert in Vienna, forcing the pop star to cancel three performances, is sentenced to 15 years behind bars.

items.title: Labor campaigned on 'integrity' so the NACC should be enjoying public trust by now
items.summary: In an extraordinary irony, the National Anti-Corruption Commission has become best known for "own goals", rocked by internal scandals and attracting a litany of complaints.

items.title: Government faces questions about first payment to Nauru under NZYQ deportee deal
items.summary: Details of the secretive NZYQ resettlement deal have emerged, including a breakdown of the government's first payment.

items.title: Warning issued as UK hits 35C in spring and records tumble
items.summary: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.

items.title: In the outback town of Wilcannia, hope lies in the classroom and on Country
items.summary: Schools in the outback often struggle to maintain consistent attendance rates, but the Wilcannia community is going the extra mile to get kids into the classroom.

items.title: Treasury boss says top income earners would have paid $400,000 more in tax if Labor plan was in place
items.summary: Commonwealth Treasury secretary Jenny Wilkinson says the average top income earner benefited from the existing investment tax arrangements to the tune of $700,000 over their lifetime, versus just $5,700 for median income earners.

items.title: Australians have 'startlingly' low awareness of controllable dementia risk factors
items.summary: Too many Australians still see dementia as an inevitability, according to new data, but there is a list of steps you can take to reduce the risks.

items.title: Small business Yarn'n says Labor's capital gains tax plan 'handcuffs' ambition
items.summary: Small business owners are urging Labor to rethink more than just the eligibility thresholds for capital gains tax concessions.

items.title: How to grow garlic in a small garden
items.summary: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

items.title: Johnny Galvatron left his band to make video games. Now, Mixtape explores 90s nostalgia through music
items.summary: Melbourne's Johnny Galvatron gave up the rockstar life to become a BAFTA-winning game developer. Now, his studio's latest release, Mixtape, re-imagines the music game as an eclectic playlist of genres and mechanics.

items.title: ABC news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback
items.summary: Your hopes of passing might come crashing down like so many drones in a harbour. But it's still worth a try.

items.title: Trump says Murdoch indicated he would 'handle' newspaper's Epstein story
items.summary: In refiling his lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal, the US president alleges that Rupert Murdoch indicated that a story about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein would not be published.

items.title: Iran fires missile at US air base in Kuwait in retaliation for American attack
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he is not yet satisfied by negotiations to end the war in Iran, and the US is not discussing easing sanctions, which is one of Tehran's key demands.

items.title: AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett defends Ben Roberts-Smith airport arrest
items.summary: The Australian Federal Police has detailed intelligence surrounding Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest, pushing back against criticisms of its public nature.

items.title: People trapped in new housing estate traffic bottlenecks welcome funding
items.summary: Motorists in Melbourne's outer north welcome funds for "lifeline" outer suburbs road, but say more needs to be done.

items.title: Former police officer says he got into bed with woman but denies raping her, court hears
items.summary: A former South Australian police officer says he got into bed with a woman while she was sleeping and caressed her thigh soon after she rejected his advances, but denies raping her, a court has heard.

items.title: Coronial inquest probes NSW Police staffing levels and mental health support
items.summary: An inquest into the death of a NSW Police recruit who was less than three months into the job hears differing opinions about whether it was related to his work.

items.title: Hawks to go to AFL over Watson's disallowed goal
items.summary: Hawthorn expects a quick AFL adjustment after Nick Watson's goal at half-time was disallowed against St Kilda in the Hawks' win.

items.title: Diver describes extreme and hostile claustrophobia inside the Laos cave
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Cave diver reveals how missing Laos group was found as South Australian joins rescue efforts
items.summary: A diver describes the "emotional moment" when he found a group of men alive after they were trapped for more than a week in a Laos cave, while a South Australian renowned for his ability to navigate tight spaces joins the rescue mission.

items.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: 'Unfair' Women's Origin timing criticised as Blues sweep Maroons aside
items.summary: The captains of New South Wales and Queensland question the timing of the Women's State of Origin series after the Blues completed an historic clean sweep.

items.title: State of Origin referee Ashley Klein's decision to send off Kalyn Ponga backed by NRL
items.summary: The NRL supports Ashley Klein's controversial decision to send off Kalyn Ponga, saying the State of Origin referee got the call right.

items.title: Inquest finds RAAF serviceman Gary Sargeant's death was preventable
items.summary: A coroner has found that the death of an RAAF serviceman could have been prevented, including when he left a military health centre during a mental health crisis. Warning: This story contains discussion of suicide and mental health issues.

items.title: Brisbane's $3.6b Olympic stadium faces court challenge by residents
items.summary: The Queensland government wants site work to begin on Brisbane's $3.6 billion Olympic stadium on Monday as residents plan a court challenge and Indigenous protesters refuse to move.

items.title: Man who killed Darwin mum Sheena Fairfield indefinitely detained due to mental impairment
items.summary: Sheena Fairfield was stabbed to death outside her family home more than three years ago. Her killer will now spend 20 years in supervised custody.

items.title: Victoria's political donations cap to double under proposed Electoral Act changes
items.summary: The cap on political donations in Victoria will be at least doubled and public funding for political parties increased under government proposals to rewrite the regime struck out by the High Court.

items.title: Canavan lines up another stunt as Albanese lays a tax trap
items.summary: This week a few people got a grilling at Senate estimates, the tax debate heated up and whispers of a new centre-right party fluttered through the press gallery.

items.title: Lawyers flag legal action over Tiwi Island town manager's decision to block bail of alleged murderer
items.summary: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

items.title: Hudson Young finds method in the madness for long-awaited State of Origin arrival
items.summary: It took three years and four series, but when Hudson Young was given the chance to impose his unorthodox abilities on State of Origin football, he didn't hesitate to harness the chaos for New South Wales.

items.title: Investigation finds smuggled drugs cause of inmate Phi Long Dang's death at Fulham prison
items.summary: A 35-year-old inmate at Victoria's Fulham Correctional Centre died of a drug overdose after consuming drugs smuggled into the jail, a coronial investigation has found.

items.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate with over $5 trillion to be bequeathed in coming decades
items.summary: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

items.title: Police treating fatal unit fire in Canberra's north as a homicide, victim identified
items.summary: ACT Policing is treating the death of Kylee McHugh, 46, as a homicide and calling for people to come forward if they had recently seen her.

items.title: Lawyer says alleged childcare paedophile Joshua Brown will admit to crimes
items.summary: Joshua Brown, who has been charged with 156 offences, including sexual assault, has appeared in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court via video link.
